task_spec.goal: Use the FR3 robot arm to insert a peg into a socket and analyze alignment failures.
constraints: Keep insertion axis close to vertical.
Stop if lateral contact force exceeds threshold.

experiment_plan.hypothesis: Changing key controllable variables will affect insert success rate and failure modes.

variables.name: lateral_offset
variables.reason: Vary lateral_offset to test sensitivity for insert.

variables.name: insertion_angle
variables.reason: Vary insertion_angle to test sensitivity for insert.

variables.name: compliance
variables.reason: Vary compliance to test sensitivity for insert.

experiment_plan.expected_result: The run should produce success/failure labels, task metrics, and an actionable next experiment.
risk_notes: This plan is a baseline experiment plan; it should not be interpreted as a trained policy.
Low sample counts are smoke-test evidence only.

preconditions: Target pose is inside the robot workspace.

expected_observations: Pose error decreases before contact action.

preconditions: Fixture and inserted part are aligned within tolerance.
expected_observations: Sustained contact without large lateral displacement.

preconditions: Robot has a gripper or detachable tool state.
expected_observations: Gripper opens and object is no longer constrained.

confidence_notes: Sample count is 3; treat the result as a smoke-test signal, not a generalization claim.
Failure distribution is based on runner-reported failure_type labels.
next_actions: Inspect the dominant failure type and run a retry plan with narrowed parameters.

retry_plan.reason: Contact-rich insertion or screwdriving is sensitive to pose error.

revised_task_spec.goal: Use the FR3 robot arm to insert a peg into a socket and analyze alignment failures.
constraints: Keep insertion axis close to vertical.
Stop if lateral contact force exceeds threshold.

task_spec.goal: Use the FR3 robot arm to press a button target on the table.
constraints: Keep contact force within the simulated safe range.
Do not collide with non-target objects.

experiment_plan.hypothesis: Changing key controllable variables will affect press success rate and failure modes.

variables.name: approach_height
variables.reason: Vary approach_height to test sensitivity for press.

variables.name: downforce
variables.reason: Vary downforce to test sensitivity for press.

experiment_plan.expected_result: The run should produce success/failure labels, task metrics, and an actionable next experiment.
risk_notes: This plan is a baseline experiment plan; it should not be interpreted as a trained policy.
Low sample counts are smoke-test evidence only.

preconditions: Target pose is inside the robot workspace.
Target is a pressable contact object.
expected_observations: Contact steps and normal force exceed threshold.

preconditions: Robot has a gripper or detachable tool state.
expected_observations: Gripper opens and object is no longer constrained.

confidence_notes: Sample count is 3; treat the result as a smoke-test signal, not a generalization claim.
Failure distribution is based on runner-reported failure_type labels.
next_actions: Inspect the dominant failure type and run a retry plan with narrowed parameters.

retry_plan.reason: Contact was detected but did not meet the force/duration criterion.

revised_task_spec.goal: Use the FR3 robot arm to press a button target on the table.
constraints: Keep contact force within the simulated safe range.
Do not collide with non-target objects.
